title: UPI Intent Payment Credential
description: Transaction-specific credential containing an NPCI-compliant UPI Intent URI and optional QR code data. Generated by the business (via PSP) during Complete Checkout and returned in the escalation response with error code 'requires_buyer_authentication'. The platform executes the intent_uri natively (upi:// deep link) or renders the QR code for desktop/web. The platform MUST fall back to continue_url if it cannot execute the credential.

intent_uri.description: NPCI-compliant UPI Intent URI. Must contain the 'pa' (payee VPA) parameter per NPCI UPI Linking Specification. Example: upi://pay?pa=merchant@rzp&pn=MerchantName&tr=TXN123&am=500.00&cu=INR

transaction_reference.description: Unique transaction reference (maps to NPCI 'tr' parameter). Maximum 35 characters per NPCI spec.

qr_code_data.description: Base64-encoded QR code image (data URI, e.g. data:image/png;base64,...). For desktop or web contexts where upi:// intent launching is unavailable.

qr_code_string.description: Raw QR code content string (typically identical to intent_uri). Platforms MAY use this to render their own QR image rather than decoding qr_code_data.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 expiry timestamp. Per NPCI best practice, UPI Intent URIs are valid for 5–15 minutes. Platforms MUST NOT attempt to execute an expired credential and MUST fall back to continue_url.
